dsVertClient 1.1.0
    • Overview
    • Generalised linear models
    • GLM (binomial, K=3)
    • NB (over-dispersed Poisson, K=3)
    • Survival
    • Cox PH (K=2 OT-Beaver)
    • Cox PH (K=3 Allison-Poisson trick)
    • Mixed models / longitudinal
    • LMM (K=2 closed-form REML)
    • LMM (K=3 REML profile + within-between)
    • GEE (K=3 sandwich)
    • GLMM (K=3 binomial Laplace)
    • Categorical
    • Ordinal joint Newton (K=2 Tutz)
    • Ordinal warm OVR (K=3)
    • Multinomial joint Newton (K=2 Bohning)
    • Multinomial warm OVR (K=3)
    • Causal / penalised
    • IPW (K=3 two-stage)
    • LASSO (K=3 proximal handoff)
  • Reference

Articles

Federated estimators – one vignette per method

Each article exercises a single dsVert estimator end-to-end on a canonical small public dataset, against the analyst’s go-to centralised R reference (stats::glm, survival::coxph, nlme::lme, MASS::glm.nb, MASS::polr, nnet::multinom, lme4::glmer, geepack::geeglm, weighted glm, lm/glmnet), using the in-process DSLite multi-server harness. The five-section template (context + math + non-disclosure + partition table | data preparation + DSLite stand-up + PSI alignment | federated fit | centralised reference | comparison + verdict) is identical across methods so the reader can compare estimators by skimming.

Generalised linear model — federated binomial logistic regression (K = 3)
Negative-binomial GLM — federated over-dispersed Poisson regression (K = 3)
Cox proportional-hazards — federated K=2 OT-Beaver dishonest-majority
Cox K=3 — federated Allison-1982 Poisson trick over GLM K=3
Linear mixed model — federated K=2 closed-form REML
Linear mixed model — federated K=3 REML 1-D profile + within-between ANOVA
GEE — federated Liang–Zeger sandwich estimator (K = 3)
Binomial GLMM — Laplace-approximated random-intercept logistic (K = 3)
Ordinal proportional-odds — federated Tutz joint Newton (K = 2)
Ordinal warm — federated cumulative-binomial OVR (K = 3)
Multinomial logistic — federated Bohning-bound joint Newton (K = 2)
Multinomial warm — federated OVR + softmax-anchor (K = 3)
Inverse-probability weighting — federated two-stage propensity GLM (K = 3)
LASSO — federated proximal-gradient handoff over the Gaussian GLM normal-equations (K = 3)

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
